text: Lighter shoes and barefoot running improve running economy compared with heavier shoes, with a small beneficial effect.
Minimalist shoes improve running economy compared with conventional shoes, with a small beneficial effect.
Training in minimalist shoes produces a moderate beneficial effect on running economy compared to conventional shoes.
Cushioned shoes provide a small beneficial effect on running economy compared to less cushioned alternatives.
Shoe mass is positively associated with the metabolic cost of running — heavier shoes cost more energy.
Footwear with a combined shoe mass less than 440 g per pair has no detrimental effect on running economy.
Comfortable shoes provide a trivial but statistically significant beneficial effect on running economy.
Shoes with increased longitudinal bending stiffness provide a trivial but statistically significant beneficial effect on running economy.
